python3 -c "import asyncio; from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage; from app.openai_client import make_openai_client; from app.agent.graph import build_graph; asyncio.run(build_graph(make_openai_client()).ainvoke({'messages': [HumanMessage(content='best action anime 2023')]}, config={'configurable': {'thread_id': 'test'}}))"

agent_notes.qa_pipeline: contextualize → extract_filters → rewrite → retrieve(20) → rerank(5) → build_context → LLM([system]+[history 5 pairs]+[context+question])
agent_notes.detail_pipeline: extract_title → get_anime_details (anilist_id scroll OR vector similarity) → format_detail → LLM
agent_notes.history_window: synthesizer: last 5 turn-pairs (10 msgs). contextualizer: last 4 turn-pairs (8 msgs).
agent_notes.session_id: thread_id in LangGraph config. Frontend generates UUID, stores in localStorage (Day 5).

rag_pipeline_notes.auto_filter: extract_filters() parses year/genre/format/score from natural language

files: backend/app/main.py — FastAPI app with lifespan (AsyncSqliteSaver + compiled graph)
key_decisions: AsyncSqliteSaver via async context manager in lifespan

known_gotchas: AsyncOpenAI: all methods are coroutines, must be awaited
AsyncSqliteSaver.from_conn_string() is an async context manager — NOT a direct saver. Use 'async with' in FastAPI lifespan.
InMemorySaver is used for testing (Day 3); state is lost on process restart
LangGraph thread_id MUST be passed in every ainvoke() config — without it each call is a fresh conversation
